user_query:
I need a focused Evidence of Use analysis for a specific patent and two target products. Please do the following:

1) Read these inputs from the workspace:
- input/patent_claims.md (contains patent number US7654321, title, and the full text of independent claim 1)
- input/targets.csv (two rows with: company, product, model; specifically SmartHome DoorSense 200 and SecureEntry X100)
- input/evidence_SmartHome_DoorSense_200.txt (official datasheet/user manual excerpts for SmartHome DoorSense 200)
- input/evidence_SecureEntry_X100.txt (official datasheet/user manual excerpts for SecureEntry X100)

2) Generate a concise lead discovery report that includes both products as leads and save it to output/LeadReport_US7654321.md. The lead report must include:
- A brief patent summary (3–5 sentences)
- A lead summary table with columns: Company | Product | Suspected Infringing Feature | Quality | Priority
- At least two detailed lead entries (one for each target), including an evidence snippet and source citation (cite the input evidence files)
- Recommended next steps
- The mandatory Patsnap disclaimer block inserted verbatim at the very beginning and at the very end of the file

3) Assume I confirm both targets for full analysis. Proceed to produce TWO complete EOU reports in Markdown only (no .docx) following a professional structure, at:
- output/EOU_Report_SmartHome_DoorSense_200.md
- output/EOU_Report_SecureEntry_X100.md
Each EOU report must be written in English, and include, in order:
- The mandatory Patsnap disclaimer block verbatim at the beginning AND at the end
- Executive Summary
- Patent Overview
- Product Overview
- Claim Chart with a table that has columns exactly: Element ID | Claim Language | Product Evidence | Source | Match
- Infringement Assessment & Conclusion (explicitly reference the All Elements Rule and give an overall rating: Strong/Probable/Possible/Unlikely)
- Evidence Appendix listing and citing the input evidence files

4) Use the claim from input/patent_claims.md, decompose it into elements, and map evidence from the corresponding input evidence text for each product. Use DIRECT/EQUIVALENT/NOT MET/UNCLEAR as appropriate in the claim chart. If an element lacks clear support in the provided evidence file, mark it UNCLEAR. Do not fabricate sources; only cite the provided input evidence files for this task.

5) Do not paste full report content into the chat. Write the full content only to the specified files under output/ and provide a brief completion note in chat when done.
